meta.title: Atlante della Governance dell'IA
meta.note: claims[].sources resolved from sources.csv (S-ids); documents resolved from sources_v2 via node_sources (SRC-ids). Both inline — no id-juggling needed.

glossary.plain_definition: What the official rules say — regardless of whether they are enforced.
What actually happens — how much the rules are really applied.
The rules are ambitious but enforcement lags, and everyone can see it.
More happens in practice than the rules require — usually through courts and lawsuits.
Systems must be approved before launch, instead of corrected afterwards.
Compliant on paper while practice quietly diverges, out of sight.
The real situation cannot be seen from outside — deliberately.
A voluntary promise a company can quietly change or drop.
Little gap because there is not much binding rule in place yet.
An organization that uses AI (not the company that builds it) and is responsible for how it is used.
A company building the largest, most capable AI systems.
A model that can be used for many different tasks.
The rules exist, but the people and tools to enforce them are not built yet.
A window after a law takes effect before penalties apply.
A simple five-step scale: almost none · minimal · partial · substantial · comprehensive.
Read directly (verified) · a real document we could not open (attested) · reasoned from evidence (inferred).
The specific future event that would prove a prediction wrong.
A rule that an AI service must be filed and accepted before it can be deployed.

markers.causal_chain: Pre-deployment filing -> deployment conditional on acceptance -> governance enforced at the gate before market -> realization structurally coupled to deployment -> stable because enforcement is built into the pipeline
markers.confound: 'Authoritarian state with administrative capacity' predicts, registry incidental
markers.discriminating_counterfactual: EU GPAI register: notification-not-gate (no pre-deployment acceptance required) -> EU governance LAGS at M1, not stable-realization like China's gate
markers.verdict: CAUSAL - prediction tracks the gate not the registry
markers.ex_ante_marker: Is deployment legally conditional on a filing being accepted? Yes -> M3 (stable). Notification-only -> not M3 (expect lag)

markers.causal_chain: No external enforcement -> continuation depends on committer discretion -> discretion sensitive to incentive/leadership shifts -> withdrawn at near-zero cost at any shift
markers.confound: The political transition caused revocation, not the voluntary form
markers.discriminating_counterfactual: 2x2: voluntary+shock (US federal EO) DIED in hours; binding+shock (US state statutes) SURVIVED the same federal turn (Cruz moratorium fell 99-1, EO cannot preempt); voluntary+no-shock (lab Seoul commitments) ERODED with no shock (12/20 published, xAI missed twice)
markers.verdict: CAUSAL - confound answered on both axes; voluntariness breeds fragility independent of the shock
markers.ex_ante_marker: Is there external enforcement, or is the commitment self-declared/self-revisable? Self-declared -> M6 (variable durability, erodes even without a shock)

markers.causal_chain: Ambitious binding instrument adopted BUT realization needs apparatus that doesn't exist AND gap openly acknowledged -> binding constraint is capacity not will -> phase-ins, delays, incremental build, eventual partial realization
markers.confound: Mere appearance of an instrument-realization gap is also produced by ceremonial adoption (no intent to realize)
markers.discriminating_counterfactual: NYC LL144: ambitious binding instrument, ~2 complaints in 2 years, realization FLAT not converging (which is why it is M4-submerged not M1). EU contrast: delay CAUSED by missing CEN-CENELEC standards, build underway -> converging-with-friction
markers.verdict: CAUSAL but counterfactual is the thinnest (no clean national-scale ceremonial adopter yet)
markers.ex_ante_marker: Is the gap openly acknowledged AND is there an observable build (standards in progress, bodies designated, timelines slipping-but-moving)? Yes -> M1 (grinding convergence). No build + no acknowledgment -> ceremonial (flat hollowness)

markers.causal_chain: Instrument-light posture that is a deliberate chosen equilibrium -> reflects a settled political choice not a transitional state -> no internal pressure builds toward binding regulation -> stability
markers.confound: Cannot tell 'deliberate' (stable) from 'nascent/legislative-pending' (transitional) except in hindsight -> unfalsifiable ('migration is the datum' dodge)
markers.discriminating_counterfactual: Vietnam (the retrodiction's clean falsifier): at t-24 had active PDPL + digital-tech legislative pipeline, benchmarked UP vs Korea/Japan, To Lam 'era of national rise', and NO stated light-touch declaration -> under the marker rule it would NOT be DLT, would be flagged enactment-capable (correct). Stayers (UK/India/Singapore/Japan) all had explicit stated light-touch choices
markers.verdict: PARTIALLY REPAIRS the central unfalsifiability - 'deliberate' becomes an observable ex-ante property
markers.ex_ante_marker: Is there an explicit on-record statement of intent to remain light AND no active comprehensive-legislation pipeline? Both -> M7-DLT (stability). Either fails -> not DLT; default developmental/nascent (no stability prediction)

legitimacy_edges.justification: Shared human-dignity / human-centeredness core (also VN-OECD, VN-COE) — they share a floor, not rival camps
Rights + rule of law, division of labour (OECD framework / CoE binding form) — predicts soft principles hardening into law
Brussels (precaution/rights) vs Washington (market/innovation) — the central intra-democratic tension; explains EU/US divergence as a legitimacy clash (mechanism)
Universal claim vs civilizationally-grounded (also vs Beijing) — predicts where universal consensus is thin (endorsed but differently realized)
Catastrophic-risk foresight vs development/inclusion — explains why frontier-safety framings under-mobilize Global-South states (cross-reference)
Rome Call catalysed corporate ethics commitments (IBM/Microsoft/Cisco/Qualcomm signing) — legitimacy -> actor-intent
UNESCO RAM catalysed national strategies in 40+ states — why Kenya/Nigeria/South Africa resemble one another (M7 nascent co-driver)
Same UNESCO-RAM common cause of the nascent-cohort resemblance
Same UNESCO-RAM common cause of the nascent-cohort resemblance
OECD Hiroshima/HAIP reporting framework channels the actor-layer frameworks (METR-12 publishers report under it)
CoE Framework Convention crystallizing into the EU's paper-C hard law (EU ratified 15 May 2026)
EU intent draws authority from Brussels-rights + Council of Europe (rights-juridical)
US intent draws authority from Washington (market/innovation + security)
China intent draws authority from Beijing (developmental-sovereigntist) — endorses but differently realizes UNESCO-universal
UAE/Saudi intent draws on Islamic-civilizational + Global-South-developmental + Rome Call (triple source) — why M7 is developmental-promotional with a values overlay
Anthropic intent draws authority from AI-safety/longtermist vision
Financial-services model-risk deployer draws authority from the prudential-regulation tradition — why it is the highest-realization node
True shared 'transparency' term BUT recording every transparency-overlap explodes combinatorially and adds no distinct inference — convergent-core edge already carries it
Bilateral overlap derivable from the convergent core (E01) — adds no decision value

nodes.name: European Union
nodes.headline: The big rulebook is real, but the machinery to enforce it is running late.
nodes.summary: The EU passed the world's most comprehensive AI law, but the hardest obligations were pushed to 2027–2028 and the technical standards aren't ready. The rules exist; the capacity to apply them is still being built.
nodes.morphology_plain: Open gap — ambitious on paper, lagging in practice.
nodes.notes: High-risk obligations deferred to 2027/2028 (Digital Omnibus 7 May 2026); standards not ready before Q4 2026 — capacity-lag with build underway

claims.claim_text: High-risk obligations deferred to 2 Dec 2027 / 2 Aug 2028 by the Digital Omnibus political agreement; standards not ready before Q4 2026

nodes.name: United States (federal)
nodes.headline: Federal AI policy can flip overnight — and it did.
nodes.summary: There is no standing federal AI law; direction is set by executive orders the next administration can reverse. The Biden-era order was rescinded within hours of the 2025 transition, and the posture turned deregulatory.
nodes.morphology_plain: Self-imposed and reversible — a private promise that can be dropped.
nodes.notes: Biden EO 14110 rescinded hours into Trump term (EO 14148, 20 Jan 2025); EO 14179 + EO 14365 deregulatory turn

claims.claim_text: Biden EO 14110 rescinded within hours (EO 14148, 20 Jan 2025); EO 14179 issued 23 Jan 2025

nodes.name: China
nodes.headline: Cleared before launch — and what slips through stays out of sight.
nodes.summary: China controls AI upfront: services must be filed and approved before going live, so rules and practice line up at the gate. Where use breaks the rules it is hidden rather than open — official compliance on the surface, quiet workarounds underneath.
nodes.morphology_plain: Controlled upfront — systems cleared before launch.
nodes.notes: Realization S via the registration gate (anti-prior: zero sanctions is not absence); Labeling Measures eff. 1 Sep 2025, CSL AI provision 1 Jan 2026

claims.claim_text: Realization S via registration gate; Labeling Measures effective 1 Sep 2025; CSL AI provision effective 1 Jan 2026; sustained CAC filing enforcement

nodes.name: United Kingdom
nodes.headline: Deliberately light — oversight is left to existing regulators.
nodes.summary: The UK has chosen not to pass a broad AI law, distributing oversight across existing regulators instead. This is a settled choice, not a transition.

nodes.name: US HR / employment deployer

nodes.morphology_plain: Inversion — stronger in practice than on paper, via courts.
